zh.Kinnersley Castle, Hereford, Herefordshi: 英國英格蘭賀福爾金納斯利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley於1382年出生在英國賀福爾時，他的父親Kinard de La Bere11379歲，母親Alice Monington33歲。他和Joan West至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1422年在英國賀福爾維奧布利享年40歲。

zh-hans.Kinnersley Castle, Hereford, Herefordshi: 英国英格兰賀福爾金納斯利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley于1382年出生在英国賀福爾时，他的父亲Kinard de La Bere11379岁，母亲Alice Monington33岁。他和Joan West至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1422年在英国賀福爾維奧布利享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: LA
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: BERE,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Beer  .

variants.name: Beer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erhard, Hans, Heinz, Aloys, Bodo, Erna, Ernst, Frieda, Friedhelm, Hanni.

English (West Country): habitational name from any of the forty or so places in southwestern England called Beer(e) or Bear(e). Most of these derive their names from the West Saxon dative case, beara, of Old English bearu ‘grove, wood’ (the standard Old English dative bearwe being preserved in Barrow ). In some cases the surname may be topographic in origin, from atte beare ‘at the grove’. Some may be from Middle English bere ‘woodland swine-pasture’ (Old English bǣr). Compare Bear 1.
English: variant of   Bear   2 ‘bear’.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Kinnerseley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Kinnersley (Herefs, Shrops, Surrey, Worcs).

variants.name: Kindersley
meanings: English:  see  Kinnersley  .

variants.name: Kynnersley
meanings: English:  see  Kinnersley  .

variants.name: Kinnersly
meanings: English:  see  Kinnersley  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley was born in 1382, in Herefordshire, England, his father, Kinard de La Bere, was 11379 and his mother, Alice Monington, was 33. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Joan West. He died in 1422, in Weobley, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley est né en 1382, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Kinard de La Bere, avait 11379 ans et sa mère, Alice Monington, avait 33 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Joan West. Il est décédé en 1422, à Weobley, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley 1382 in Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Kinard de La Bere 11379 und seine Mutter Alice Monington 33. Er hatte mit Joan West mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1422 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Weobley, Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley è nato il nel 1382 nel Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Kinard de La Bere, aveva 11379 anni e il suo madre, Alice Monington, aveva 33 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Joan West. Morì nel 1422, nel Weobley, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 40 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley(이)가 1382년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Kinard de La Bere(은)는 11379세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Monington(은)는 33세였다. 그는 Joan West(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1422년 영국 허퍼드실르 w오블리에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley nasceu em 1382, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Kinard de La Bere, tinha 11379 anos e sua mãe, Alice Monington, tinha 33 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de LA BERE, Lord of Kinnerseley родился в 1382 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Kinard de La Bere было 11379 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Monington, 33. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Joan West. Он умер в 1422 г. в местности веобли, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Joan West. Murió en 1422, en Weobley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años.
